findings.title: DIRECTIVE-20260519-001 (third-inbox-writer audit) completed: PROP-046-amendment-002 authored.
findings.details: Performed comprehensive attention-inbox writer-site audit per directive task_1. Exhaustive grep across monitor/prompts/, monitor/prompts/reference/, monitor/scripts/, build-scripts/ with five distinct search patterns (literal 'attention-inbox', append-write patterns, ATT- ID prefixes, pushed_by literals, dispatcher reads). Cross-checked against attention-inbox-archive.jsonl observed item shapes. Found exactly THREE creator writers: W1 = decider-intake.md ~L240 (STALE-ASSESS), W2 = analyst-mode1-expansions.md ~L70 (PROP029-ORPHAN-DETECTED), W3 = decider.md ~L378-389 (ATT-*). W1+W2 already covered by PROP-046-amendment-001. W3 is the missed third writer (the 'I patched WIN/section/prediction; analyst please re-verify' template). Confirmed via archive evidence: ATT-2026-05-18T05:24:22.762Z and ATT-2026-05-18T09:26:17.253Z (the 2 stuck items mentioned in directive bug_summary) both carry `pushed_by:'decider'` with no `target_mode` field — matches W3 template exactly. Chose amendment-002 over new PROP-049 because: (a) directive's task_2 test fires (only 1 additional site, not 5+); (b) operator preference for minimal patches; (c) amendment-001 still pending apply, so amendment-002 bundles with it for one apply action; (d) PROP-048 already claimed by tinker-2026-05-19T12-05.
findings.recommended_action: Operator: review PROP-046-amendment-002 and apply alongside amendment-001 (one Edit-tool patch each). 5-10 min total apply time.
findings.evidence: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-046-amendment-002-third-writer.json (1 patch, 6 counter-arguments, 6 regression-check items, 4 out-of-scope followups). DIRECTIVE-20260519-001 marked status=completed in FUSE.

One directive remains git-only: DIRECTIVE-20260518-002 (clone-depth audit, medium). The structural fix (workspace-sync git→FUSE pull for append_only_glob directories, OR tinker pre-flight git-side directive check) is still not implemented. Each tinker run continues to fix-by-curl one directive at a time. This is operationally working but is technical debt.
findings.recommended_action: Operator: decide between option (a) workspace-sync directional extension or option (b) tinker pre-flight git-side directive check (12:05 run recommended (b) as smaller scope). File PROP-049 (or PROP-050) authorizing the chosen option, then implement. As a stopgap, future tinker runs should curl-list operator-directories on github at pre-flight and reconcile against FUSE listing.

findings.title: build-scripts/generate-html.js: 1-line caption diff persists.
findings.details: Workspace has 'plus 1 analyst-added claim (WIN-070)' in the catalog-entries caption (~line 500); GitHub does not. WIN-070 was the analyst-added claim referenced; whether it currently exists in data/wins.json should be checked before deciding which direction is correct. Persistent across 12:05 → 02:40 → 12:43 runs (no further drift observed; same byte-diff each time). This is dormant — generate-html.js is regenerated on every build.js publish, so any future publish that includes the more-recent template will resolve it.
findings.recommended_action: Defer to next Mode 3 audit. Low-priority because file is regenerated on publish and current rendered HTML is unaffected (template hash-only diff).

findings.title: Project-footprint disk warning fires every run because FUSE workspace IS the project.
findings.details: Mode 2 disk check threshold is 'project-induced footprint >=500MB'. Current footprint 741MB breakdown: 723MB FUSE workspace (the repo itself) + ~18MB other (mostly poller scratch /tmp/dome-*.html). The threshold was sized for 'FUSE workspaces + ephemeral agent clones + agent scratch' but the workspace alone is 700MB+. Result: WARNING fires every run with no actionable response. Standing /tmp-clone cleanup empowerment (24h-old clones) has nothing to clean. The threshold should either (a) exclude the FUSE workspace size from the computed footprint, or (b) raise the absolute threshold to >800MB so it only fires on actual clone leaks.
findings.recommended_action: Next Mode 3 / Mode 4 run: file a small PROP or self-fix to tinker.md Mode 2 disk-check block to subtract workspace baseline before applying threshold. Tiny scope, possibly self-fixable per tinker.md 'Conservative self-fixes' rule.
findings.evidence: du -sm output: /sessions/festive-epic-cerf/mnt/dome-model-review = 723MB, all other paths under /tmp = ~18MB combined.

findings.title: Tinker fired 3x in <24h (02:40, 12:05, 12:43) vs documented 'Daily ~10:30 AM' schedule.
findings.details: Tinker schedule has drifted from the documented daily-cron toward higher frequency (3 runs in last 10h). The 12:05 → 12:43 gap is only 38 minutes — within the duplicate-Mode-1-concurrency window that PROP-044 (analyst-claim-discipline) addresses for analyst. The 12:05 run took ~12min; this run started ~25min after the 12:05 finished, so no overlap with in-flight work, but the cadence implies some scheduling change (operator-triggered or bumped cron). The pipeline-health table in tinker.md still says 'Daily ~10:30 AM' which is stale.
findings.recommended_action: Verify actual cron via mcp__scheduled-tasks__list_scheduled_tasks; if cron has been intentionally bumped, update tinker.md pipeline-health table to reflect current cadence. If cron is still daily, this run is an operator-trigger and the table is correct; flag for follow-up.
